danger_signals.visual_form: Visible bullet sprites with distinct coloring (typically bright/contrasting hues)
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat requiring evasion or counter-fire

danger_signals.visual_form: Large, mechanically complex sprite designs (e.g., Maximum Jumbo giant robot) that dominate screen space
danger_signals.meaning: High-threat scenario requiring pattern recognition and sustained combat

danger_signals.visual_form: Grotesque, asymmetrical creature designs distinct from human soldiers
danger_signals.meaning: Exotic threat type; unpredictable behavior patterns (as opposed to military discipline)

danger_signals.visual_form: Stage-specific obstacles (platforms, moving terrain, implied by level design)
danger_signals.meaning: World-state danger independent of enemy presence

danger_signals.visual_form: Weapon inventory UI change after respawn; visual downgrade in firepower capability
danger_signals.meaning: Mechanical vulnerability state; temporary weakening of player capacity

safety_signals.visual_form: Flying capsule pods with labeled markers (A, B, C, D type designations)
safety_signals.meaning: Power-up availability; progression toward enhanced combat capability

safety_signals.visual_form: Solid ground/walkable surfaces with consistent visual grounding
safety_signals.meaning: Safe traversal zone; reduced environmental threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Sliding animation (diagonal-downward movement input); invulnerable-state visuals (likely flashing or color shift)
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary damage immunity; tactical advantage window

safety_signals.visual_form: Bomb counter UI persistence post-respawn (unlike weapons which are lost)
safety_signals.meaning: Strategic resource preservation; safety net mechanic

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Hard Corps unit designation/insignia (not documented in sources)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Military base/urban defense settings; characterized by task-force deployment scenarios

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Alien cell bio-organic imagery; renegade military aesthetic
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mix of human soldiers and bio-engineered creatures created from stolen alien cell
Humanoid alien redesign (especially in PAL/Probotector version)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Scientific facility aesthetic; merger machine imagery
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Bio-organic weapon creatures produced by merger experiments
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Laboratory/research facility stages; experimental bio-weapon containment

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Post-Alien Wars recovery (5 years after Contra III)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Urban/military infrastructure under siege; blend of human technology and lingering alien threat presence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Task force deployment; increased militarization; terrorist activity escalation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bio-organic creatures; mutation imagery; laboratory hazmat aesthetics; grotesque enemy designs
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy variety increases in later stages; unpredictable creature behavior vs. disciplined soldier tactics

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage path divergence; alternative boss encounters; faction-specific environmental redesigns
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player choices materialize as different enemy compositions and final-stage scenarios

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Political instability (Colonel Bahamut as renegade scientist)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Militarized zones; infected/contaminated areas; surveillance of research facilities
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mix of military enemies and bio-created threats reflecting internal power struggle

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character selection (4 unique operatives: Ray, Sheena, Brad Fang cyborg, Browny robot)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Team-based narrative; differing visual designs signify specialized roles; co-op multiplayer faction identity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Vehicle transformation sequences; hoverbike-to-ostrich-robot morph animations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mobility-enhanced combat zones; altered stage pacing and threat presentation

environmental_storytelling.example: Character dialogue reveals Colonel Bahamut's identity and motivation mid-stage; alien cell theft plot exposition
Maximum Jumbo (Stage 1 sub-boss) revealed via cutscene to be piloted by a human operator, suggesting military/scientific control of bio-tech
Four playable characters (human commando, cyborg, combat robot) visually communicate Hard Corps as specialized multi-disciplinary unit
Early stages feature human soldiers; later stages introduce bio-organic creatures, mapping escalation of alien cell weaponization
Player choices (e.g., alliance with Alien General) manifest as entirely different level layouts and boss encounters, visualizing political consequence
Laboratory stages vs. urban combat zones vs. alien-origin environments use distinct visual language to communicate faction territory/influence
A/B/C/D weapon slots (character-specific) suggest customized loadouts reflecting specialized Hard Corps operative roles

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Disciplined movement patterns; coordinated projectile fire; predictable patrol routines
Erratic movement; varied attack patterns; reflect mutation/loss of control themes
Multi-phase attack patterns; telegraphed mega-attacks; dominate screen real-estate; require tactical positioning
Temporary invulnerability frames; offensive capability (can harm certain enemies during slide); momentum-based movement
Hoverbike fast-movement; robot transformation capability; altered collision/damage properties
Passive flight trajectory; collection-triggered power-up; labeled visual identification system
Directional presence in story; faction orchestration; cutscene agency (drives plot branching)

notes: Contra: Hard Corps uses character diversity (Ray, Sheena, Brad Fang cyborg, Browny robot) as primary faction identity marker rather than color-coding. Branching narrative structure and multiple endings (including optional alliance with Alien General) create dynamic world-state visualization where player choice directly alters enemy composition, stage layouts, and final-stage aesthetic. PAL/Probotector localization demonstrates regional world-state variation (robotic character replacements, alien redesigns, X-Drive plot substitution). The game communicates danger through enemy variety escalation (human soldiers → bio-organic hybrids) and visual complexity of boss designs. Safety is signaled through weapon pickup systems and invulnerability mechanics (sliding state). Environmental storytelling relies heavily on cutscene exposition combined with enemy type progression to map alien cell weaponization timeline.
